title: Keiko's Big Day at the Park
synopsis: Keiko feels excited to visit the park with Mama, but when she loses her favorite toy bunny, she becomes very sad and scared. With help from kind strangers and Mama's comfort, Keiko learns that even on difficult days, there are people who care and want to help her feel better.

pages.text: Keiko feels very happy today! She holds her soft bunny toy tight. "We're going to the park!" says Mama. Keiko jumps up and down. The sun is shining bright. Keiko loves the park so much.

illustration.description: A cheerful Japanese-American toddler girl with short black hair in pigtails, wearing a bright yellow dress, holding a small white bunny toy. She's jumping excitedly next to her mother, a Japanese woman in casual clothes. They stand by a front door with sunlight streaming in. The scene uses warm yellows and oranges with a textured, construction paper-like background.
illustration.alt: Happy Keiko jumping with her bunny toy while Mama watches

pages.text: At the park, Keiko runs to the swings. "Push me higher, Mama!" she giggles. The wind makes her hair fly. Other children play nearby. A boy with curly hair waves hello. Keiko waves back with her bunny.

illustration.description: Keiko on a bright red swing, laughing with joy as her mother pushes her. Her bunny toy sits in her lap. In the background, diverse children play - a Black boy with curly hair waves, and other children of different ethnicities play on colorful playground equipment. The scene is vibrant with primary colors against a blue sky and green grass texture.
illustration.alt: Keiko swinging happily at the playground with other children nearby

pages.text: After swinging, Keiko runs to the sandbox. She puts her bunny down to build a castle. Sand flies everywhere! Keiko laughs and plays. But when she looks up... her bunny is gone! "Mama!" Keiko calls. "Where is my bunny?"

illustration.description: Keiko kneeling in a golden sandbox, looking around with worried eyes and hands on her cheeks. Sand toys are scattered around. The empty spot where her bunny was is clearly visible. Her expression shows the beginning of concern. The sandbox has warm tan colors with bright plastic toys in primary colors.
illustration.alt: Keiko realizing her bunny toy is missing from the sandbox

pages.text: Keiko feels very sad now. Tears roll down her cheeks. "My bunny!" she cries. Mama hugs her tight. "Don't worry," says Mama gently. "We will look everywhere. Many people here can help us find your bunny."

illustration.description: Keiko crying with big tears on her round cheeks, being comforted by her mother's warm embrace. Mama has a gentle, caring expression. The color palette shifts to softer blues and purples to show Keiko's sad feelings, but Mama's warm presence adds touches of comforting pinks and oranges.
illustration.alt: Mama comforting sad Keiko with a gentle hug

pages.text: A kind lady with gray hair smiles at Keiko. "I saw a white bunny by the slide," she says. The boy from before runs over too. "I'll help you look!" he says. Keiko feels a little brave now. Maybe they can find bunny together!

illustration.description: An elderly white woman with kind eyes and gray hair pointing toward the playground slide. The friendly Black boy with curly hair stands nearby with an eager, helpful expression. Keiko looks up hopefully, wiping her eyes. The scene brightens with warmer colors showing hope and community support.
illustration.alt: Kind strangers offering to help Keiko find her lost bunny

pages.text: They all search together. Under the slide... no bunny. By the see-saw... no bunny. Keiko feels scared she'll never find it. Then the helpful boy shouts, "Look! In the flowers!" There is Keiko's bunny, safe in the soft grass!

illustration.description: The small group searching together - Keiko, Mama, the elderly lady, and the boy all looking in different directions. The white bunny toy is visible nestled among bright colored flowers (reds, yellows, blues) in a garden bed. Everyone's faces show excitement at the discovery. The scene bursts with celebratory primary colors.
illustration.alt: Everyone finding Keiko's bunny toy among the colorful flowers

pages.text: Keiko runs to get her bunny. She hugs it so tight! "Thank you! Thank you!" she says to everyone who helped. Keiko feels happy again. She learned that when she's sad or scared, there are always kind people ready to help. What a wonderful day at the park!

illustration.description: Keiko beaming with joy, squeezing her bunny toy against her chest. Around her, the helpful strangers smile warmly - Mama, the elderly lady, and the boy all looking pleased. The scene glows with bright, warm colors - sunny yellows, happy oranges, and cheerful reds, showing the joy of community and friendship.
illustration.alt: Happy Keiko reunited with her bunny, surrounded by helpful friends

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How does Keiko feel at the beginning of the story?
questions.explanation: Keiko feels very happy because she's going to the park with Mama!

questions.question: What does Keiko lose at the park?
questions.explanation: Keiko loses her favorite bunny toy while playing in the sandbox.

questions.question: Who helps Keiko look for her lost toy?
choices: A kind lady and a boy
questions.explanation: A kind lady with gray hair and a friendly boy both offer to help Keiko search.

questions.question: Where do they find Keiko's bunny?
questions.explanation: The helpful boy finds Keiko's bunny safe in the flowers and soft grass.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Page 1
pages.text: Keiko feels very _____ today! She holds her soft _____ toy tight. At the park, Keiko runs to the _____.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Page 2
pages.text: When Keiko can't find her bunny, she feels very _____. _____ hugs her tight. Kind people _____ Keiko look for her bunny.
